mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the unemployment rate below 5% signals a tight labor market that boosts corporate profit margins and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in 60%+ of instances where UNRATE < 5%, or the correlation between UNRATE and SPX returns falls below 0.2 in rolling 1-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in payrolls (PAYEMS) and declining unemployment (UNRATE) signals a virtuous cycle of employment expansion and wage growth, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between PAYEMS and UNRATE fails to explain 15%+ of SPX return variation in out-of-sample tests, or the combined signal produces negative returns in 40%+ of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in industrial production (INDPRO) above 95 signals strengthening manufacturing activity, which drives corporate earnings growth and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX returns in INDPRO > 95 regimes show negative or flat performance in 50%+ of cases, or the signal's IC drops below 0.1 in 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A housing starts (HOUST) level above 900 thousand units indicates strong demand for residential construction, boosting related sectors and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX returns in HOUST > 900 regimes show negative or flat performance in 50%+ of cases, or the signal's IC drops below 0.1 in 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: During monetary tightening cycles, rising inflation (CPIAUCSL) above 230 correlates with bond yield increases that pressure equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction between CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS fails to explain 15%+ of SPX return variation in out-of-sample tests, or the combined signal produces positive returns in 40%+ of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the federal funds rate above 2% signals tighter monetary policy that raises discount rates and reduces equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in 60%+ of instances where FEDFUNDS > 2%, or the signal's IC drops below 0.1 in 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly GDP growth (GDPC1) above 1% indicates strong macroeconomic momentum that supports equity earnings, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX returns in GDPC1 > 1% regimes show negative or flat performance in 50%+ of cases, or the signal's IC drops below 0.1 in 3-year rolling windows.
